Top — all years

      Dissertations over time

      All assigned dissertations by year. Click a bar to jump to that year.

      Methods & Sources
      Department assignment — three-source pipeline

      30,662 of 32,013 records (95.8%) carry a mapped modern department. Departments were assigned from three sources, applied in priority order:

      1. ProQuest Dissertations & Theses Global — 6,310 records (2009–2025) have departments directly from ProQuest. These are the only records where department data was entered at source; pre-2010 ProQuest records were retrodigitised with title and date only. Source: ProQuest PQDT Global, exported via TDM Studio.
      2. UChicago Convocation Programs, 1932–2009 (convocation_data.xlsx) — 22,993 records matched to ProQuest by title+year (case-insensitive, whitespace-collapsed). 97% match rate by exact title; 40-character prefix fallback recovers an additional ~363 records where titles differ in punctuation or subtitle handling. Column width varies by era: 9-column rows (pre-~1990) store department in column 8; 10-column rows (~1990–2009) store it in the last column. We always read the last column and fall back to the second-to-last (division) when the last is empty. Source: University of Chicago Library dissertation and convocation office archives.
      3. HathiTrust Digitized Catalog, 1893–1931 (hathi_1893-1931.csv) — 2,617 records with department and division fields. Strategy A (title match): 1,499 records matched by title+year (exact or 40-char prefix). Strategy B (author+year match): when title strategies fail, the student's surname is extracted from the ProQuest author field ("LASTNAME, FIRSTNAME" format) and matched against HathiTrust's "FIRSTNAME LASTNAME" field for the same graduation year. Only unambiguous single matches are accepted; ties (two authors with the same surname in the same year) are silently discarded to prevent misassignment. This recovered an additional 869 records. Source: HathiTrust Digital Library digitized catalog (hathitrust.org), via UChicago Library.

      Remaining unknowns (1,351 records, 4.2%): Primarily records where no supplementary source matched, or where the source listed only a division (e.g., "Division of Social Sciences") too broad to assign to a specific department. The Graduate Library School (141 records, 1926–1989) is mapped to Harper Memorial Library (1926–1969) then Joseph Regenstein Library (1970–1989). Home Economics (72 records, 1918–1956) is mapped to 5740 S. Woodlawn Ave from 1929, confirmed by CEHD building history. Radiology and medical-school clinical departments are excluded — they were on the medical campus outside the academic quad covered by this visualization.

      Department normalization & building history
      All 44 canonical departments — historical names, building timelines, judgment calls, and sources — are in the Dept Histories tab → Click any bar to open the full department card.

      Year

      All
      — dissertations with geo focus

      Top countries

        Shifts over time — top 6 countries

        Dissertation count by decade, all available years.

        Methods & Sources
        Geographic NER pipeline

        Geographic research focus is extracted using spaCy en_core_web_lg Named Entity Recognition on each dissertation's Title, Paper Keywords, and Subject Terms. GPE and LOC entities are collected, then normalized to ISO country names via a 200+ entry alias table covering historical state names (USSR → Russia, Persia → Iran), major cities (London → United Kingdom, Beijing → China), ancient entities (Babylonia → Iraq, Ottoman Empire → Turkey), and UK constituent nations. US states and US cities are excluded to avoid inflating the US count from domestic references. Each country is counted once per dissertation (deduplicated).

        No abstract field: ProQuest did not include abstracts in the export. Coverage is limited to dissertations where the title or keywords name a place — a structural limitation of the TDM export. 4,068 of 32,013 records (12.7%) have a detectable geographic entity. Top countries: United States (379), China (239), France (166), UK (165), India (148).

        Sources
        • ProQuest PQDT Global — dissertation metadata (title, keywords, subject terms)
        • Explosion AI / spaCy — en_core_web_lg NER model (OntoNotes 5.0)
        • Natural Earth via datasets/geo-countries — world country polygons (1:50m GeoJSON)

        Keyword Similarity Ring

        How to read this diagram
        How to Read: Ring Diagram
        Nodes Each dot is a department. Order around the ring follows a clockwise campus tour built from angular sort around campus center and refined with 2-opt smoothing, so the loop stays geographically coherent while reducing long jumps. Color = academic division (legend below). Ring edges Arcs connect consecutive ring neighbors. Color and thickness are normalized to this ring's min/max similarity range so the full scale is always visible: red = weakest, blue = mid-range, green = strongest. A red arc between nearby buildings suggests spatial proximity without much shared vocabulary in that period. Hover chords Mouse over a node to reveal maroon chords connecting it to every department it shares keyword overlap with, not just its ring neighbors. Chord thickness = cosine similarity. The tooltip shows ring-neighbor scores and the single strongest non-adjacent connection. Ring break The reddest, thinnest arc marks the weakest consecutive link in the displayed ring. The breakpoint label below names both departments and gives their similarity score and distance. Year slider Recomputes the ring for the selected year using only departments with enough keyword data in that year to build a similarity matrix. The tour order, edge colors, and breakpoint are recalculated for that yearly network. Sparse early years may not have enough data to draw a full ring. ⇌ Campus Morphs ring positions into actual building locations on the underlying campus map — lets you verify that the tour order matches real geography and that the ring break occurs between genuinely proximate buildings.

        Year

        All time
        Edge similarity weak
        strong

        Similarity Network

        Force-directed graph: nodes cluster by keyword similarity. Edge weight = cosine similarity. Drag nodes freely. Color = academic division. Switch to Scatter for distance vs. similarity.

        How to read · Analysis · Methods & Sources
        How to Read: Right Panel Views
        Scatter Axes X = haversine distance between building centroids (meters, ~50–2,000 m). Y = cosine similarity of TF-IDF keyword vectors (0–1). A downward slope would indicate proximity correlates with similarity. Dot color Same-division pairs use their division color; cross-division pairs are grey. Network Nodes / edges Each circle is a department (color = division). Lines connect pairs with cosine similarity ≥ 0.08; thickness scales with strength. Drag nodes freely. Node set mirrors the active ring year. Hover Highlights a node's edges in maroon; tooltip shows its three most similar departments and scores. Time Series Line Mean pairwise cosine similarity across all department pairs per year. Rising = vocabulary converging across campus; falling = increasing specialization. Gold dot marks the active ring year. Hover for exact values.

        Top Keywords by Department

        Select a department to see its TF-IDF top terms (all-time corpus).

        Methods

        Keyword corpus construction For each department, all dissertations assigned to it contribute their Paper Keywords and Subject Terms fields from the ProQuest record. These are concatenated into a single keyword document per department (pool-of-words model). Title text is excluded — titles are too short and noisy for reliable topic signals. Departments with fewer than 30 total keyword terms are excluded from the ring. For per-decade matrices, the threshold is 15 terms.
        TF-IDF vectorization and cosine similarity Each department's keyword document is vectorized with scikit-learn TfidfVectorizer: up to 800 features, English stop words removed, minimum document frequency 2, unigrams only. TF-IDF down-weights terms that appear in almost every department (common academic vocabulary) and up-weights terms distinctive to a specific field. Pairwise cosine similarity between the resulting unit vectors produces a symmetric similarity matrix (up to 44×44, filtered to depts with sufficient keywords). Values range [0, 1]. Per-year matrices use the same method but restricted to dissertations from that year (300 features, min_df=1 due to smaller corpora). Decade matrices are also generated for summary views and historical comparison.

        Drives: ring edge color/width · hover chord thickness · scatter Y-axis · network edge weight · ring breakpoint · keyword display · yearly network · time series line
        Spatial distance and campus tour order Building positions are the geometric centroid of each department's current building polygon from the UChicago Property Footprint GeoJSON (Facilities Services, 2025). Pairwise distance is the haversine great-circle distance in meters. Campus range: ~50 m (Zoology Building ↔ Anatomy Building) to ~2,000 m (Art History ↔ Public Policy).

        Ring order is determined by a clockwise angular sort around the campus center, followed by 2-opt improvement to reduce local crossings and long jumps. The result is a geographic tour of campus where physically adjacent buildings appear as ring neighbors — the prerequisite for the ring species test.

        Drives: ring node order · scatter X-axis · breakpoint distance label · campus morph layout · force simulation collision radius
        D3 force graph simulation The network layout uses D3 v7 forceSimulation with four forces: a link force attracting connected pairs with strength proportional to cosine similarity and distance inversely proportional to similarity (so highly similar departments pull together tightly); a many-body repulsion preventing overlap; a centering force; and a collision radius of 16 px. The simulation runs until convergence and remains live — dragging a node reactivates it. Only pairs above the similarity threshold (0.08) are drawn as edges. The year shown matches the year slider; the node set mirrors the active ring departments.
        Data provenance 30,662 / 32,013 records (95.8%) carry a mapped department, filled from three sources: ProQuest (6,310 direct), UChicago Convocation Programs 1932–2009 (~22,993 matched by title+year), and HathiTrust Digitized Catalog 1893–1931 (~2,617, with author+year fallback). Some individual years remain too sparse to build a yearly keyword matrix; those years appear as gaps or reduced-node rings rather than silently falling back to all-time values. Full pipeline detail and department cards are in the Dept Histories tab →
        Data sources
        • ProQuest Dissertations & Theses Global — 32,013 UChicago records, 1893–2025
        • UChicago Property Footprint GeoJSON (Facilities Services, 2025) — building polygons and construction dates used for campus distance and morph
        • scikit-learn — TfidfVectorizer and cosine_similarity
        • D3.js v7 — force-directed network layout
        • spaCy en_core_web_lg — geographic NER (Tab 2 only)
        • Natural Earth geo-countries — world choropleth polygons (Tab 2 only)
